Space Packing Architecture

The Life and Work of Alfred Neumann

Context
The Life and Work of Alfred Neumann
Venue
Cabinet of Architecture, Ostrava
Subject
Alfred Neumann
Links
Space Packed ArchDaily

'Space Packing Architecture: The Life and Work of Alfred Neumann' is an exhibition that showcases the work of Czech architect Alfred Neumann. The exhibition focuses on Neumann's major design projects in Israel during the 1960s, which he worked on with collaborators Zvi Hecker and Eldar Sharon. Neumann's approach to architecture departed from the canonical International Style and embraced accreted patterns of spatial units and polyhedral geometries to create innovative and sustainable buildings. The exhibition features architectural models and life-size spatial units that illustrate Neumann's vision for a new kind of modern architecture.

For more information, you can explore the 'Space Packed' publication and the ArchDaily article on Alfred Neumann's life and work.

Ariel Noyman

City Science Center, MIT Media Lab noyman [at] mit [dot] edu 75 Amherst Street, Cambridge, MA 02139
